vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely unsupported by visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) and P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are not visible as established specimens. Only P3 (Pothos) is identifiable via the white rabbit anchor.

plant_audit.P1: No foliage detected. Soil surface appears undisturbed.
plant_audit.P2: No foliage detected. Soil surface appears undisturbed.
plant_audit.P3: Single green shoot/leaf visible. Appears to be a solitary Pothos cutting or sprout.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: White particulate matter (likely perlite or mineral deposits) present on the surface of P3.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-2: No significant change; pots remain largely barren.
temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: The single green shoot in P3 remains stable in position and posture.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of extreme dormancy or failure. Only P3 shows signs of life (a single green shoot). The lack of growth in P1, P2, and P4 suggests either total specimen loss or a failure to germinate/establish.
vision_report.anomalies: The registry suggests a multi-plant environment, but the visual reality is a near-empty desk with only one viable sprout.
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a meticulous audit of the provided image sequence. The environment is characterized by a lack of expected botanical diversity. P3 is the only pot containing a visible plant specimen—a single, small green shoot. The other pots (P1, P2, P4) are devoid of visible plant matter, indicating a systemic loss of the registered specimens. The soil appears consistently moist, yet no further growth or germination has occurred over the 5-day observation window.
